incorrects.description: Student set Runner C bar to wrong height on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Runner C completed 35 laps. Find where 35 is on the axis.
Look at the ghost gridlines. Runner C needs 35. Where is that between the lines?

incorrects.description: Student set Runner C bar to wrong height on second attempt

highlight_axis_values.description: Axis values 30, 35, 40 highlight. Ghost gridline at 35 pulses.
Axis marks at 30, 35, and 40 highlight. Ghost gridline at 35 pulses.

params.text: 35 is halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 is 35. Set the bar to end at that line.
35 is halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 equals 35. Set the bar to that height.

steps.description: Student set Runner C bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets Runner C bar to 35, halfway between 30 and 40 lines.
System demonstrates setting Runner C bar to height 35. Bar grows from 0 to 35, stopping at the ghost gridline halfway between 30 and 40.

steps.text: Here's how this works. I look at the data table: Runner C completed 35 laps. I find 35 on the axis. It's between the 30 line and the 40 line, exactly halfway. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 is 35. The bar ends at 35. That's how you match the bar height to the data when the value is between the lines.

Let me show you. Runner C completed 35 laps. I find 30 on the axis, then 40. Halfway between them is 35. The bar ends right there at the middle line. That's how you place a bar between the axis lines.
